content: ALWAYS update the ROADMAP.md file when completing development tasks.

### ✅ Mark as Complete When:
1. **Feature is fully implemented and tested**
2. **All tests pass**
3. **Code is deployed/merged**
4. **Documentation is updated**

### 📝 Update Progress When:
1. **Starting work on a roadmap item**
2. **Completing sub-tasks within a larger item**
3. **Encountering blockers or delays**
4. **Changing scope or approach**
Before starting any work, check if the task relates to a roadmap item.
Use these status indicators: `[ ]` - Not started, `[~]` - In progress, `[x]` - Completed, `[!]` - Blocked/Issues
When marking items complete, add: ✅ **COMPLETED** marker, Date completed, Brief description of what was accomplished, Links to relevant files/documentation

When following TDD workflow, automatically check if the completed feature relates to roadmap items: Search roadmap for related keywords, Update status if match found, Add completion details, Update phase progress if applicable
Update phase completion percentages. Calculate based on completed vs total items in the phase.
Before Starting Work: Check ROADMAP.md for related items, Update status to "In Progress" if found, Note start date and approach
During Development: Update sub-task progress as items are completed, Note any scope changes or blockers, Add relevant file references
After Completing Work: Mark items as complete with ✅ marker, Add completion date, Update phase progress percentage, Add links to relevant documentation
Weekly Reviews: Update progress on all in-progress items, Identify blockers and dependencies, Adjust timelines if needed
Monthly Reviews: Review phase completion percentages, Update target dates if needed, Add new items discovered during development
Quarterly Reviews: Major roadmap restructuring if needed, Update success metrics and targets, Plan next quarter's priorities
Automatically Track: Test coverage percentages, Feature completion rates, Bug fix completion times, Performance improvements
Update Metrics When: Completing major features, Reaching phase milestones, Monthly reviews
The roadmap is a living document that should always reflect: Current project status, Accurate completion percentages, Realistic timelines, Active work in progress, Known blockers and issues.
Update it with every significant development milestone!
